      "content": "1) Backend FastAPI : Sécurité “prod-grade” (CSP + CSRF + Rate limit)\n1.1 backend/middlewares/security_headers.py\nfrom starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware\nfrom starlette.responses import Response\nimport secrets\n\nclass SecurityHeadersMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):\n    async def dispatch(self, request, call_next):\n        nonce = secrets.token_urlsafe(16)\n        response: Response = await call_next(request)\n\n        csp = \"; \".join([\n            \"default-src 'self'\",\n            f\"script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}'\",\n            \"style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\",\n            \"img-src 'self' data: https:\",\n            \"connect-src 'self' https:\",\n            \"font-src 'self' data:\",\n            \"object-src 'none'\",\n            \"base-uri 'self'\",\n            \"frame-ancestors 'none'\",\n        ])\n\n        response.headers[\"Content-Security-Policy\"] = csp\n        response.headers[\"X-Content-Type-Options\"] = \"nosniff\"\n        response.headers[\"Referrer-Policy\"] = \"strict-origin-when-cross-origin\"\n        response.headers[\"Permissions-Policy\"] = \"geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()\"\n        response.headers[\"X-Nonce\"] = nonce\n        return response\n\n1.2 backend/security/csrf.py (double-submit pour SPA)\nimport secrets\nfrom fastapi import Request, HTTPException\n\nCSRF_COOKIE = \"csrf_token\"\nCSRF_HEADER = \"x-csrf-token\"\n\ndef mint_csrf_token() -> str:\n    return secrets.token_hex(32)\n\ndef require_csrf(request: Request):\n    # Only enforce on state-changing methods\n    if request.method in (\"GET\", \"HEAD\", \"OPTIONS\"):\n        return\n\n    cookie = request.cookies.get(CSRF_COOKIE)\n    header = request.headers.get(CSRF_HEADER)\n\n    if not cookie or not header or cookie != header:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=\"CSRF check failed\")\n\n1.3 backend/routers/csrf_router.py\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter\nfrom fastapi.responses import JSONResponse\nfrom backend.security.csrf import mint_csrf_token, CSRF_COOKIE\n\nrouter = APIRouter(tags=[\"security\"])\n\n@router.get(\"/api/csrf\")\ndef get_csrf():\n    token = mint_csrf_token()\n    res = JSONResponse({\"csrfToken\": token})\n    # cookie readable by JS (double-submit)\n    res.set_cookie(\n        CSRF_COOKIE, token,\n        httponly=False, secure=True, samesite=\"none\", path=\"/\"\n    )\n    return res\n\n1.4 backend/security/rate_limit.py (Redis option)\n\nSi tu as déjà Redis (souvent le plus clean pour une API publique), fais simple :\n\nimport time\nfrom fastapi import Request, HTTPException\nimport redis\n\nr = redis.Redis.from_url(\n    url=str(__import__(\"os\").environ.get(\"REDIS_URL\", \"\")),\n    decode_responses=True\n)\n\ndef rate_limit(request: Request, key_prefix: str, max_calls: int, window_sec: int):\n    ip = request.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\", request.client.host).split(\",\")[0].strip()\n    path = request.url.path\n    bucket = int(time.time() // window_sec)\n    key = f\"rl:{key_prefix}:{ip}:{path}:{bucket}\"\n\n    count = r.incr(key)\n    if count == 1:\n        r.expire(key, window_sec + 2)\n\n    if count > max_calls:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=429, detail=f\"Rate limit exceeded. Retry in ~{window_sec}s.\")\n\n\nSi tu n’as pas Redis, je te donne une version in-memory (moins robuste en multi-instance), mais Redis est “la voie royale” en prod.\n\n2) Brancher ces briques dans ton backend/main.py\n\nDans ton FastAPI() :\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\nfrom backend.middlewares.security_headers import SecurityHeadersMiddleware\nfrom backend.routers.csrf_router import router as csrf_router\n\napp = FastAPI()\n\napp.add_middleware(SecurityHeadersMiddleware)\n\napp.include_router(csrf_router)\n\n\nPuis sur les endpoints “mutations” (calculate, update, crawl, admin), tu ajoutes CSRF + rate-limit.\n\nExemple sur ton endpoint de calcul :\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Request, Depends\nfrom backend.security.csrf import require_csrf\nfrom backend.security.rate_limit import rate_limit\n\nrouter = APIRouter()\n\n@router.post(\"/api/calculate-tariff\")\ndef calculate_tariff(request: Request):\n    require_csrf(request)\n    rate_limit(request, key_prefix=\"calc\", max_calls=30, window_sec=60)\n    # ... ton traitement\n\n3) Worker Queue : industrialiser “crawl / refresh datasets” (MongoDB-friendly)\n\nVu que ton backend est FastAPI + MongoDB , le pattern le plus pragmatique est :\n\nMongoDB = persistance des jobs (crawl_jobs)\n\nRQ (Redis Queue) ou Celery = exécution asynchrone\n\nWorker = processus séparé (scale horizontal facile)\n\n3.1 backend/jobs/models.py (Mongo schema logique)\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field\nfrom typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Literal\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nStatus = Literal[\"queued\",\"running\",\"done\",\"failed\",\"stopped\"]\n\nclass CrawlJob(BaseModel):\n    job_id: str\n    status: Status = \"queued\"\n    params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)\n    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None\n    ended_at: Optional[datetime] = None\n    log_path: Optional[str] = None\n    error: Optional[str] = None\n\n3.2 backend/jobs/queue.py (RQ)\nimport os\nfrom rq import Queue\nfrom redis import Redis\n\nredis_conn = Redis.from_url(os.environ[\"REDIS_URL\"])\ncrawl_queue = Queue(\"crawl\", connection=redis_conn)\n\n3.3 backend/jobs/tasks.py\nimport os, pathlib, traceback\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom backend.db.mongo import db  # adapte à ton module Mongo\nfrom backend.crawlers.dza_tariff_connector import run_crawl  # ton crawler\n\nLOG_DIR = pathlib.Path(\"worker-logs\")\n\ndef crawl_task(job_id: str, params: dict):\n    col = db[\"crawl_jobs\"]\n    LOG_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n    log_path = str(LOG_DIR / f\"{job_id}.log\")\n\n    col.update_one({\"job_id\": job_id}, {\"$set\": {\"status\":\"running\",\"started_at\": datetime.utcnow(), \"log_path\": log_path}})\n\n    try:\n        run_crawl(params=params, log_path=log_path)\n        col.update_one({\"job_id\": job_id}, {\"$set\": {\"status\":\"done\",\"ended_at\": datetime.utcnow()}})\n        return True\n    except Exception as e:\n        col.update_one({\"job_id\": job_id}, {\"$set\": {\"status\":\"failed\",\"ended_at\": datetime.utcnow(), \"error\": str(e), \"trace\": traceback.format_exc()}})\n        return False\n\n3.4 backend/routers/crawl_router.py (enqueue)\nimport uuid\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Request\nfrom backend.security.csrf import require_csrf\nfrom backend.security.rate_limit import rate_limit\nfrom backend.jobs.queue import crawl_queue\nfrom backend.db.mongo import db\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nrouter = APIRouter(tags=[\"crawl\"])\n\n@router.post(\"/api/crawl/new\")\ndef new_crawl(request: Request, payload: dict):\n    require_csrf(request)\n    rate_limit(request, key_prefix=\"crawl\", max_calls=5, window_sec=60)\n\n    job_id = uuid.uuid4().hex\n    db[\"crawl_jobs\"].insert_one({\n        \"job_id\": job_id,\n        \"status\": \"queued\",\n        \"params\": payload,\n        \"created_at\": datetime.utcnow()\n    })\n\n    crawl_queue.enqueue(\"backend.jobs.tasks.crawl_task\", job_id, payload, job_id=job_id)\n    return {\"job_id\": job_id, \"status\": \"queued\"}\n\n3.5 Worker run\n\nDans un terminal/VM :\n\nrq worker crawl\n\n4) Frontend React : CSRF bootstrap + header automatique\n\nAu boot de ton app (ex : src/lib/api.ts ou src/main.tsx) :\n\nexport async function initCsrf() {\n  await fetch(\"/api/csrf\", { credentials: \"include\" });\n}\n\n\nEt sur chaque POST/PUT/DELETE :\n\nfunction getCookie(name: string) {\n  return document.cookie.split(\"; \").find(r => r.startsWith(name + \"=\"))?.split(\"=\")[1] ?? \"\";\n}\n\nexport async function postJson(url: string, body: any) {\n  const csrf = getCookie(\"csrf_token\");\n  return fetch(url, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    credentials: \"include\",\n    headers: {\n      \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n      \"x-csrf-token\": decodeURIComponent(csrf),\n    },\n    body: JSON.stringify(body),\n  });\n}\n\n5) Dépendances à ajouter\nBackend\npip install redis rq\n\n\nVariables d’env :\n\nREDIS_URL=redis://...\n\n6) Ce que je te conseille comme “merge plan” (propre sur GitHub)\n\nfeat/security-headers-csrf-ratelimit\n\nfeat/queue-rq-worker-crawl\n\nfeat/qa-delta-pipeline (on branchera tes scripts QA/delta ensuite sur la fin de job)",

Let me provide some feedback and help you organize this work effectively:\n\n## 🎯 Implementation Strengths\n\n1. **Security Headers Middleware** - Solid CSP with nonce-based script execution\n2. **CSRF Protection** - Double-submit pattern works well for SPAs\n3. **Rate Limiting** - Redis-based solution scales horizontally\n4. **Background Jobs** - RQ + MongoDB is a clean, pragmatic choice\n5. **Clear separation of concerns** across modules\n\n## 📋 Suggested Implementation Order\n\n### Phase 1: Security Foundation\n**Branch:** `feat/security-headers-csrf-ratelimit`\n\n**Files to create:**\n- `backend/middlewares/security_headers.py`\n- `backend/security/csrf.py`\n- `backend/routers/csrf_router.py`\n- `backend/security/rate_limit.py`\n- Update `backend/main.py`\n- Frontend: `src/lib/csrf.ts` or update `src/lib/api.ts`\n\n**Key considerations:**\n- ✅ CSRF enforcement on mutations only (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH)\n- ⚠️ CSP `script-src` with nonces might break existing inline scripts - test thoroughly\n- ⚠️ `samesite=\"none\"` requires `secure=True` (HTTPS only)\n- 💡 Consider adding CORS configuration if frontend is on different domain\n\n### Phase 2: Worker Queue\n**Branch:** `feat/queue-rq-worker-crawl`\n\n**Files to create:**\n- `backend/jobs/models.py`\n- `backend/jobs/queue.py`\n- `backend/jobs/tasks.py`\n- `backend/routers/crawl_router.py`\n- `worker-logs/` directory (add to `.gitignore`)\n\n**Additional suggestions:**\n```python\n# Add to backend/jobs/tasks.py for better observability\nimport logging\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\ndef crawl_task(job_id: str, params: dict):\n    logger.info(f\"Starting crawl job {job_id} with params: {params}\")\n    # ... your rate limit logic\n        return True\n    return dependency\n\n# Usage:\n@router.post(\"/api/calculate-tariff\", dependencies=[Depends(rate_limit_dependency(30, 60))])\n```\n\n### 3. **Environment Variables**\nCreate `.env.example`:\n```bash\nREDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379\nMONGODB_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017\nENVIRONMENT=production\nCORS_ORIGINS=https://yourdomain.com\n```\n\n### 4. **Docker Compose for Local Dev**\n```yaml\n# docker-compose.yml\nversion: '3.8'\nservices:\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n  \n  worker:\n    build: .\n    command: rq worker crawl\n    depends_on:\n      - redis\n    env_file: .env\n```\n\n## 📝 Next Steps\n\nWould you like me to:\n\n1. **Create GitHub issues** for each feature branch with detailed implementation checklists?\n2. **Create a pull request** for any of these features (I can scaffold the files)?\n3. **Review existing code** in your repository to see what's already in place?\n4. **Generate a more detailed architecture diagram** showing how these components interact?\n\nLet me know which action would be most helpful, and I'll proceed accordingly!",

Je recommande:\n\n#### 1.1 **Créer un système de versioning des données tarifaires**\n\n```python\n# backend/data/tariff_versions.py\n\"\"\"\nSystème de versioning pour les données tarifaires ZLECAf\n\"\"\"\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict, Any\n\nclass TariffVersion:\n    def __init__(self, version: str, effective_date: datetime, source: str):\n        self.version = version\n        self.effective_date = effective_date\n        self.source = source\n        self.tariff_data = {}\n    \n    def validate_against_previous(self, previous_version: 'TariffVersion') -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Compare avec version précédente et identifie les changements\"\"\"\n        changes = {\n            \"added\": [],\n            \"modified\": [],\n            \"removed\": []\n        }\n        # Logique de comparaison\n        return changes\n```\n\n#### 1.2 **Enrichir les données avec sources officielles manquantes**\n\nVos scripts utilisent déjà World Bank, OEC, UN COMTRADE. Ajoutez:\n\n```python\n# backend/data_sources/afcfta_official.py\n\"\"\"\nConnecteur vers les sources officielles AfCFTA\n\"\"\"\nimport aiohttp\n\nclass AfCFTAOfficialAPI:\n    \"\"\"\n    Scraper/API pour:\n    - AfCFTA e-Tariff Portal: https://etariff.au-afcfta.org/\n    - UNCTAD TRAINS: https://trains.unctad.org/\n    - WTO Tariff Database: https://tao.wto.org/\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    SOURCES = {\n        \"afcfta_etariff\": \"https://etariff.au-afcfta.org/api/v1\",\n        \"unctad_trains\": \"https://trains.unctad.org/api\",\n        \"wto_tariffs\": \"https://api.wto.org/timeseries/v1/data\"\n    }\n    \n    async def fetch_country_schedule(self, country_code: str, year: int):\n        \"\"\"Récupère le schedule tarifaire officiel d'un pays\"\"\"\n        # Implementation\n        pass\n    \n    async def fetch_rules_of_origin(self, hs_code: str):\n        \"\"\"Récupère les règles d'origine officielles\"\"\"\n        pass\n```\n\n### **Phase 2: Système de Crawling Automatisé avec Validation**\n\n#### 2.1 **Architecture proposée**\n\n```\nbackend/\n├── crawlers/\n│   ├── __init__.py\n│   ├── base_crawler.py          # Classe de base abstraite\n│   ├── afcfta_etariff.py        # Crawler e-Tariff Portal\n│   ├── unctad_trains.py         # Crawler UNCTAD TRAINS\n│   ├── worldbank_wdi.py         # Crawler World Bank (existant à améliorer)\n│   ├── customs_authorities.py   # Scraper sites douanes nationaux\n│   └── validators/\n│       ├── tariff_validator.py  # Validation des tarifs\n│       ├── consistency_check.py # Cohérence inter-sources\n│       └── regulatory_check.py  # Conformité réglementaire\n│\n├── jobs/\n│   ├── __init__.py\n│   ├── models.py                # Modèles MongoDB pour jobs\n│   ├── queue.py                 # RQ Queue setup\n│   ├── tasks.py                 # Tâches asynchrones\n│   └── scheduler.py             # Planification (APScheduler)\n│\n├── data/\n│   ├── tariffs/\n│   │   ├── v2024_q1.json       # Versions horodatées\n│   │   ├── v2024_q2.json\n│   │   └── current.json -> v2024_q2.json\n│   ├── regulations/\n│   │   ├── rules_of_origin.json\n│   │   ├── customs_procedures.json\n│   │   └── trade_agreements.json\n│   └── validation_reports/\n│       ├── 2024-02-01_validation.json\n│       └── latest.json\n```\n\n#### 2.2 **Crawler de base avec validation**\n\n```python\n# backend/crawlers/base_crawler.py\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, List\nimport hashlib\nimport asyncio\nimport aiohttp\n\nclass BaseCrawler(ABC):\n    def __init__(self, source_name: str, base_url: str):\n        self.source_name = source_name\n        self.base_url = base_url\n        self.session = None\n        self.last_crawl = None\n        self.data_hash = None\n    \n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        self.session = aiohttp.ClientSession()\n        return self\n    \n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):\n        await self.session.close()\n    \n    @abstractmethod\n    async def fetch_data(self, **params) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Méthode abstraite pour récupérer les données\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    async def validate_data(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Valide les données récupérées\"\"\"\n        validation_result = {\n            \"is_valid\": True,\n            \"errors\": [],\n            \"warnings\": [],\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        }\n        \n        # Validation basique\n        if not data:\n            validation_result[\"is_valid\"] = False\n            validation_result[\"errors\"].append(\"Empty data returned\")\n            return validation_result\n        \n        # Calculer hash pour détecter changements\n        data_str = str(sorted(data.items()))\n        new_hash = hashlib.sha256(data_str.encode()).hexdigest()\n        \n        if self.data_hash and new_hash == self.data_hash:\n            validation_result[\"warnings\"].append(\"No changes detected since last crawl\")\n        \n        self.data_hash = new_hash\n        return validation_result\n    \n    async def crawl_and_validate(self, **params) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Pipeline complet: crawl + validation\"\"\"\n        try:\n            data = await self.fetch_data(**params)\n            validation = await self.validate_data(data)\n            \n            return {\n                \"source\": self.source_name,\n                \"data\": data,\n                \"validation\": validation,\n                \"crawled_at\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\n                \"source\": self.source_name,\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"crawled_at\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n            }\n```\n\n#### 2.3 **Crawler spécifique pour AfCFTA e-Tariff**\n\n```python\n# backend/crawlers/afcfta_etariff.py\nfrom .base_crawler import BaseCrawler\nfrom bs4 import BeautifulSoup\nimport asyncio\n\nclass AfCFTAeTariffCrawler(BaseCrawler):\n    def __init__(self):\n        super().__init__(\n            source_name=\"AfCFTA e-Tariff Portal\",\n            base_url=\"https://etariff.au-afcfta.org\"\n        )\n    \n    async def fetch_data(self, country_code: str, hs_chapter: str = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Récupère les données tarifaires depuis le portail officiel\n        \"\"\"\n        url = f\"{self.base_url}/schedules/{country_code}\"\n        if hs_chapter:\n            url += f\"/{hs_chapter}\"\n        \n        async with self.session.get(url) as response:\n            if response.status != 200:\n                raise Exception(f\"HTTP {response.status}: {await response.text()}\")\n            \n            html = await response.text()\n            soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')\n            \n            # Parser la structure HTML du portail\n            tariff_data = self._parse_tariff_table(soup)\n            \n            return {\n                \"country_code\": country_code,\n                \"hs_chapter\": hs_chapter,\n                \"tariffs\": tariff_data,\n                \"effective_date\": self._extract_effective_date(soup)\n            }\n    \n    def _parse_tariff_table(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> List[Dict]:\n        \"\"\"Parse le tableau des tarifs\"\"\"\n        tariffs = []\n        # Logique de parsing spécifique au site\n        return tariffs\n    \n    def _extract_effective_date(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extrait la date d'effet des tarifs\"\"\"\n        # Logique d'extraction\n        return datetime.now().isoformat()\n```\n\n### **Phase 3: Système de Jobs avec RQ (Redis Queue)**\n\n#### 3.1 **Configuration de la queue**\n\n```python\n# backend/jobs/queue.py\nimport os\nfrom redis import Redis\nfrom rq import Queue\nfrom rq_scheduler import Scheduler\n\n# Connexion Redis\nredis_conn = Redis.from_url(\n    os.getenv(\"REDIS_URL\", \"redis://localhost:6379\"),\n    decode_responses=True\n)\n\n# Queues par priorité\nhigh_priority_queue = Queue('high', connection=redis_conn)\ndefault_queue = Queue('default', connection=redis_conn)\nlow_priority_queue = Queue('low', connection=redis_conn)\n\n# Scheduler pour tâches récurrentes\nscheduler = Scheduler(queue=default_queue, connection=redis_conn)\n```\n\n#### 3.2 **Modèles MongoDB pour les jobs**\n\n```python\n# backend/jobs/models.py\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field\nfrom typing import Optional, Dict, Any, Literal\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nclass JobStatus(str, Enum):\n    QUEUED = \"queued\"\n    RUNNING = \"running\"\n    DONE = \"done\"\n    FAILED = \"failed\"\n    STOPPED = \"stopped\"\n\nclass CrawlJobModel(BaseModel):\n    job_id: str = Field(..., description=\"Unique job ID\")\n    job_type: Literal[\"crawl_tariffs\", \"crawl_regulations\", \"validate_data\", \"update_stats\"]\n    status: JobStatus = JobStatus.QUEUED\n    params: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)\n    \n    # Timestamps\n    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None\n    ended_at: Optional[datetime] = None\n    \n    # Results\n    result: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n    error: Optional[str] = None\n    log_path: Optional[str] = None\n    \n    # Metadata\n    source: Optional[str] = None\n    countries_affected: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)\n    data_hash: Optional[str] = None\n    \n    class Config:\n        use_enum_values = True\n\nclass DataValidationModel(BaseModel):\n    validation_id: str\n    crawl_job_id: str\n    validation_date: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    \n    # Validation results\n    is_valid: bool\n    errors: List[Dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list)\n    warnings: List[Dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list)\n    \n    # Comparison with previous data\n    changes_detected: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)\n    \n    # Quality scores\n    completeness_score: float = 0.0  # 0-100\n    accuracy_score: float = 0.0      # 0-100\n    consistency_score: float = 0.0   # 0-100\n```\n\n#### 3.3 **Tâches asynchrones**\n\n```python\n# backend/jobs/tasks.py\nimport os\nimport pathlib\nimport traceback\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom backend.db.mongo import db  # Votre connexion MongoDB\nfrom backend.crawlers.afcfta_etariff import AfCFTAeTariffCrawler\nfrom backend.crawlers.validators.tariff_validator import TariffValidator\nimport logging\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\nLOG_DIR = pathlib.Path(\"worker-logs\")\nLOG_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\nasync def crawl_country_tariffs(job_id: str, country_code: str, hs_chapter: str = None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Tâche asynchrone pour crawler les tarifs d'un pays\n    \"\"\"\n    col = db[\"crawl_jobs\"]\n    log_path = str(LOG_DIR / f\"{job_id}.log\")\n    \n    # Update job status\n    col.update_one(\n        {\"job_id\": job_id},\n        {\"$set\": {\n            \"status\": \"running\",\n            \"started_at\": datetime.utcnow(),\n            \"log_path\": log_path\n        }}\n    )\n    \n    try:\n        # Crawl data\n        async with AfCFTAeTariffCrawler() as crawler:\n            result = await crawler.crawl_and_validate(\n                country_code=country_code,\n                hs_chapter=hs_chapter\n            )\n        \n        # Validate data\n        validator = TariffValidator()\n        validation_result = await validator.validate(result[\"data\"])\n        \n        # Save to database\n        tariff_col = db[\"tariffs\"]\n        tariff_col.insert_one({\n            **result[\"data\"],\n            \"job_id\": job_id,\n            \"validation\": validation_result,\n            \"imported_at\": datetime.utcnow()\n        })\n        \n        # Update job as done\n        col.update_one(\n            {\"job_id\": job_id},\n            {\"$set\": {\n                \"status\": \"done\",\n                \"ended_at\": datetime.utcnow(),\n                \"result\": {\n                    \"records_imported\": len(result[\"data\"].get(\"tariffs\", [])),\n                    \"validation_passed\": validation_result[\"is_valid\"]\n                }\n            }}\n        )\n        \n        logger.info(f\"Job {job_id} completed successfully\")\n        return True\n        \n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Job {job_id} failed: {str(e)}\")\n        col.update_one(\n            {\"job_id\": job_id},\n            {\"$set\": {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"ended_at\": datetime.utcnow(),\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"trace\": traceback.format_exc()\n            }}\n        )\n        return False\n\ndef schedule_daily_updates():\n    \"\"\"\n    Planifie les mises à jour quotidiennes\n    \"\"\"\n    from backend.jobs.queue import scheduler\n    \n    # Crawler tous les pays chaque jour à 2h00 UTC\n    scheduler.cron(\n        \"0 2 * * *\",  # Cron expression\n        func=crawl_all_countries,\n        queue_name='default'\n    )\n    \n    # Validation des données chaque jour à 6h00 UTC\n    scheduler.cron(\n        \"0 6 * * *\",\n        func=validate_all_data,\n        queue_name='high'\n    )\n```\n\n### **Phase 4: API Endpoints pour gérer les jobs**\n\n```python\n# backend/routers/crawl_router.py\nimport uuid\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Request, HTTPException, Depends\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\nfrom typing import Optional, List\nfrom backend.jobs.queue import default_queue\nfrom backend.db.mongo import db\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nrouter = APIRouter(tags=[\"data-crawling\"], prefix=\"/api/crawl\")\n\nclass CrawlRequest(BaseModel):\n    countries: List[str]\n    hs_chapters: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    force_refresh: bool = False\n\n@router.post(\"/tariffs\")\nasync def trigger_tariff_crawl(request: CrawlRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Lance un crawl des tarifs pour les pays spécifiés\n    \"\"\"\n    job_ids = []\n    \n    for country in request.countries:\n        job_id = f\"crawl-{country}-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}\"\n        \n        # Create job record in MongoDB\n        db[\"crawl_jobs\"].insert_one({\n            \"job_id\": job_id,\n            \"job_type\": \"crawl_tariffs\",\n            \"status\": \"queued\",\n            \"params\": {\n                \"country_code\": country,\n                \"hs_chapters\": request.hs_chapters\n            },\n            \"created_at\": datetime.utcnow(),\n            \"countries_affected\": [country]\n        })\n        \n        # Enqueue job\n        default_queue.enqueue(\n            \"backend.jobs.tasks.crawl_country_tariffs\",\n            job_id,\n            country,\n            request.hs_chapters,\n            job_id=job_id\n        )\n        \n        job_ids.append(job_id)\n    \n    return {\n        \"message\": f\"Queued {len(job_ids)} crawl jobs\",\n        \"job_ids\": job_ids\n    }\n\n@router.get(\"/jobs/{job_id}\")\nasync def get_job_status(job_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Récupère le statut d'un job\n    \"\"\"\n    job = db[\"crawl_jobs\"].find_one({\"job_id\": job_id}, {\"_id\": 0})\n    \n    if not job:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Job not found\")\n    \n    return job\n\n@router.get(\"/jobs\")\nasync def list_jobs(status: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 50):\n    \"\"\"\n    Liste les jobs récents\n    \"\"\"\n    query = {\"status\": status} if status else {}\n    jobs = list(db[\"crawl_jobs\"].find(query, {\"_id\": 0}).sort(\"created_at\", -1).limit(limit))\n    \n    return {\n        \"count\": len(jobs),\n        \"jobs\": jobs\n    }\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 **PLAN D'IMPLÉMENTATION PAR ÉTAPES**\n\n### **Sprint 1 (1-2 semaines): Fondations**\n1. ✅ Créer l'architecture des dossiers (`backend/crawlers`, `backend/jobs`)\n2. ✅ Implémenter `BaseCrawler` et `AfCFTAeTariffCrawler`\n3. ✅ Setup Redis + RQ pour la queue de jobs\n4. ✅ Créer les modèles MongoDB (`CrawlJobModel`, `DataValidationModel`)\n\n### **Sprint 2 (1-2 semaines): Crawlers & Validation**\n1. ✅ Implémenter crawlers pour chaque source (UNCTAD, WTO, douanes nationales)\n2. ✅ Créer le système de validation (`TariffValidator`, `ConsistencyChecker`)\n3. ✅ Tester sur 3-5 pays pilotes\n4. ✅ Documenter les anomalies détectées\n\n### **Sprint 3 (1 semaine): Scheduler & Automation**\n1. ✅ Implémenter le scheduler avec RQ-Scheduler ou APScheduler\n2. ✅ Créer les endpoints API (`/api/crawl/tariffs`, `/api/crawl/jobs/{id}`)\n3. ✅ Setup monitoring (logs, alertes)\n4. ✅ Dashboard simple pour visualiser les jobs\n\n### **Sprint 4 (1 semaine): Sécurité & Production**\n1. ✅ Implémenter les middlewares de sécurité (CSP, CSRF, Rate Limit)\n2. ✅ Tests de charge sur les crawlers\n3. ✅ Documentation complète\n4. ✅ Déploiement sur environnement de staging\n\n---\n\n## 💡 **QUICK WINS IMMÉDIATS**\n\nPendant que vous mettez en place l'infrastructure, vous pouvez améliorer immédiatement:\n\n### 1. **Enrichir `fix_tariffs_and_stats.py`**\n\nAjoutez les chapitres HS manquants (actuellement vous avez 01-40, 50-63, 84-89):\n\n```python\n# Ajoutez dans REAL_ZLECAF_TARIFFS\n\"41\": {\"normal\": 0.10, \"zlecaf\": 0.00, \"transition\": \"5_years\"},    # Peaux\n\"42\": {\"normal\": 0.20, \"zlecaf\": 0.00, \"transition\": \"10_years\"},   # Cuirs\n# ... jusqu'à 97\n```\n\n### 2. **Créer un script de validation des données existantes**\n\n```python\n# scripts/validate_existing_data.py\n\"\"\"\nValide toutes les données actuellement dans MongoDB\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nfrom backend.crawlers.validators.tariff_validator import TariffValidator\n\nasync def main():\n    validator = TariffValidator()\n    \n    # Valider les tarifs\n    tariffs = db[\"tariffs\"].find()\n    results = []\n    \n    for tariff in tariffs:\n        result = await validator.validate(tariff)\n        results.append(result)\n    \n    # Générer rapport\n    report = {\n        \"total_records\": len(results),\n        \"valid\": sum(1 for r in results if r[\"is_valid\"]),\n        \"invalid\": sum(1 for r in results if not r[\"is_valid\"]),\n        \"errors\": [r[\"errors\"] for r in results if not r[\"is_valid\"]]\n    }\n    \n    print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    asyncio.run(main())\n```\n\n### 3. **Améliorer les sources de données**\n\nAjoutez dans `fix_tariffs_and_stats.py` les liens directs vers les sources officielles:\n\n```python\nOFFICIAL_SOURCES = {\n    \"afcfta_etariff\": {\n        \"url\": \"https://etariff.au-afcfta.org/\",\n        \"api_endpoint\": \"https://etariff.au-afcfta.org/api/v1\",\n        \"documentation\": \"https://etariff.au-afcfta.org/docs\",\n        \"contact\": \"etariff@au-afcfta.org\",\n        \"rate_limit\": \"100 requests/day (free tier)\"\n    },\n    \"unctad_trains\": {\n        \"url\": \"https://trains.unctad.org/\",\n        \"api_endpoint\": \"https://trains.unctad.org/api\",\n        \"documentation\": \"https://trains.unctad.org/api-doc\",\n        \"contact\": \"trains@unctad.org\",\n        \"rate_limit\": \"Unlimited for non-commercial use\"\n    },\n    # ... autres sources\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 **PROCHAINES ÉTAPES RECOMMANDÉES**\n\n1. **Examiner le dossier `backend/` dans votre repo** - Je n'ai pas pu y accéder via ![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-2.0.0-blue)\n5| ![MongoDB](https://img.shields.io/badge/MongoDB-Connected-green)\n6| ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-orange)\n7| \n8| A comprehensive tariff calculator and trade information system for the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA/ZLECAf).\n9| \n10| ## 🚀 Features\n11| \n12| - **Tariff Calculations**: Calculate tariffs between 54 African countries\n13| - **Rules of Origin**: Access ZLECAf rules of origin by HS code\n14| - **Country Profiles**: Detailed economic profiles for all member states\n15| - **Trade Statistics**: Comprehensive trade statistics and projections\n16| - **Real-time Data**: Integration with World Bank, UN COMTRADE v1, and OEC APIs\n17| - **Automated Data Updates**: Daily automated updates from multiple data sources\n18| \n19| ## 📊 API Endpoints\n20| \n21| ### Health & Observability\n22| \n23| | Endpoint | Method | Description |\n24| |----------|--------|-------------|\n25| | `/api/health` | GET | Simple health check |\n26| | `/api/health/status` | GET | Detailed health status with system checks |\n27| \n28| The health endpoints provide real-time monitoring of:\n29| - Database connectivity (MongoDB)\n30| - API endpoints availability\n31| - Data integrity checks\n32| - Service version information\n33| \n34| ### Core Endpoints\n35| \n36| | Endpoint | Method | Description |\n37| |----------|--------|-------------|\n38| | `/api/` | GET | API welcome message |\n39| | `/api/countries` | GET | List all 54 ZLECAf member countries |\n40| | `/api/country-profile/{country_code}` | GET | Get detailed country economic profile |\n41| | `/api/calculate-tariff` | POST | Calculate tariffs between countries |\n42| | `/api/rules-of-origin/{hs_code}` | GET | Get rules of origin for HS code |\n43| | `/api/statistics` | GET | Get comprehensive ZLECAf statistics |\n44| \n45| ### Trade Data Endpoints (NEW)\n46| \n47| | Endpoint | Method | Description |\n48| |----------|--------|-------------|\n49| | `/api/trade-data/latest` | GET | Get latest trade data using smart source selection |\n50| | `/api/trade-data/compare-sources` | GET | Compare all data sources for freshness |\n51| | `/api/trade-data/comtrade/{reporter}/{partner}` | GET | Get UN COMTRADE bilateral trade data directly |\n52| | `/api/trade-data/wto/{reporter}/{partner}` | GET | Get WTO tariff and trade data directly |\n53| \n54| #### Examples:\n55| \n56| **Get Latest Trade Data with Smart Selection:**\n57| ```bash\n58| GET /api/trade-data/latest?reporter=KEN&partner=GHA&hs_code=080300\n59| ```\n60| \n61| **Compare Data Sources:**\n62| ```bash\n63| GET /api/trade-data/compare-sources?countries=KEN&countries=GHA&countries=TZA\n64| ```\n65| \n66| **Direct COMTRADE Access:**\n67| ```bash\n68| GET /api/trade-data/comtrade/KEN/GHA?period=2025&hs_code=080300\n69| ```\n70| \n71| **Direct WTO Access:**\n72| ```bash\n73| GET /api/trade-data/wto/KEN/GHA?product_code=080300\n74| ```\n75| \n76| ## 🏥 Health Monitoring\n77| \n78| ### Health Check Response\n79| \n80| **Endpoint**: `GET /api/health`\n81| \n82| ```json\n83| {\n84|   \"status\": \"healthy\",\n85|   \"service\": \"ZLECAf API\",\n86|   \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n87|   \"timestamp\": \"2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z\"\n88| }\n89| ```\n90| \n91| ### Detailed Status Response\n92| \n93| **Endpoint**: `GET /api/health/status`\n94| \n95| ```json\n96| {\n97|   \"status\": \"healthy\",\n98|   \"timestamp\": \"2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z\",\n99|   \"service\": \"ZLECAf API\",\n100|   \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n101|   \"checks\": {\n102|     \"database\": {\n103|       \"status\": \"healthy\",\n104|       \"message\": \"MongoDB connection active\"\n105|     },\n106|     \"api_endpoints\": {\n107|       \"status\": \"healthy\",\n108|       \"available_endpoints\": [...]\n109|     },\n110|     \"data\": {\n111|       \"status\": \"healthy\",\n112|       \"countries_count\": 54,\n113|       \"rules_of_origin_sectors\": 97\n114|     }\n115|   }\n116| }\n117| ```\n118| \n119| ## 🔧 Technology Stack\n120| \n121| - **Backend**: FastAPI (Python)\n122| - **Frontend**: React with Shadcn/UI components\n123| - **Database**: MongoDB\n124| - **External APIs**: World Bank API, OEC API, UN COMTRADE API, WTO API\n125| \n126| ## 📦 Data Sources\n127| \n128| - **World Bank** - World Development Indicators (updated daily)\n129| - **UN COMTRADE** - Most recent bilateral trade data (up to 500 calls/day free tier)\n130| - **WTO Data Portal** - Tariff and trade policy data (free access)\n131| - **OEC** - Atlas of Economic Complexity (Observatory of Economic Complexity)\n132| - **UNCTAD** - Tariff data\n133| - **AfDB** - African Economic Outlook\n134| - **IMF** - Regional Economic Outlook\n135| \n136| ### Smart Data Source Selection\n137| \n138| The API automatically selects the best data source based on:\n139| 1. **Data freshness** - Most recent data available\n140| 2. **API availability** - Rate limits and accessibility\n141| 3. **Data coverage** - Specific query requirements\n142| \n143| Priority order: **UN COMTRADE** → **OEC** → **World Bank** → **WTO**\n144| \n145| Data is automatically updated daily at 2:00 AM UTC via GitHub Actions. See [docs/AUTO_UPDATE_DATA.md](docs/AUTO_UPDATE_DATA.md) for details.\n146| \n147| ## 🌍 Coverage\n148| \n149| - **54 African Countries**\n150| - **97 HS2 Sectors** with rules of origin\n151| - **Real-time Trade Data**\n152| - **Economic Projections** through 2030\n153| \n154| ## 🚦 Status Badges Explained\n155| \n156| - ![API Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/API-Online-success) - API is operational\n157| - ![MongoDB](https://img.shields.io/badge/MongoDB-Connected-green) - Database connection active\n158| - ![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-2.0.0-blue) - Current API version\n159| \n160| ## 📈 Monitoring Best Practices\n161| \n162| 1. **Regular Health Checks**: Poll `/api/health` endpoint every 30 seconds\n163| 2. **Detailed Status**: Check `/api/health/status` for comprehensive diagnostics\n164| 3. **Database Monitoring**: Monitor MongoDB connection status\n165| 4. **API Response Times**: Track endpoint response times\n166| 5. **Error Rates**: Monitor 4xx and 5xx response rates\n167| \n168| ## 🔍 Quick Start\n169| \n170| ### Check API Health\n171| \n172| ```bash\n173| curl https://your-domain.com/api/health\n174| ```\n175| \n176| ### Get All Countries\n177| \n178| ```bash\n179| curl https://your-domain.com/api/countries\n180| ```\n181| \n182| ### Calculate Tariff\n183| \n184| ```bash\n185| curl -X POST https://your-domain.com/api/calculate-tariff \\\n186|   -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n187|   -d '{\n188|     \"origin_country\": \"KE\",\n189|     \"destination_country\": \"GH\",\n190|     \"hs_code\": \"080300\",\n191|     \"value\": 10000\n192|   }'\n193| ```\n194| \n195| ## 🔄 Automated Data Updates\n196| \n197| The system automatically updates economic data daily from external sources:\n198| \n199| - **Schedule**: Daily at 2:00 AM UTC\n200| - **Sources**: World Bank API, country profiles, trade statistics\n201| - **Process**: Automated via GitHub Actions\n202| - **Monitoring**: Update reports available in Actions artifacts\n203| \n204| For more information, see:\n205| - ![Version](https://img.shields.io/badge/version-2.0.0-blue)\n5| ![MongoDB](https://img.shields.io/badge/MongoDB-Connected-green)\n6| ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-orange)\n7| \n8| A comprehensive tariff calculator and trade information system for the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA/ZLECAf).\n9| \n10| ## 🚀 Features\n11| \n12| - **Tariff Calculations**: Calculate tariffs between 54 African countries\n13| - **Rules of Origin**: Access ZLECAf rules of origin by HS code\n14| - **Country Profiles**: Detailed economic profiles for all member states\n15| - **Trade Statistics**: Comprehensive trade statistics and projections\n16| - **Real-time Data**: Integration with World Bank, UN COMTRADE v1, and OEC APIs\n17| - **Automated Data Updates**: Daily automated updates from multiple data sources\n18| \n19| ## 📊 API Endpoints\n20| \n21| ### Health & Observability\n22| \n23| | Endpoint | Method | Description |\n24| |----------|--------|-------------|\n25| | `/api/health` | GET | Simple health check |\n26| | `/api/health/status` | GET | Detailed health status with system checks |\n27| \n28| The health endpoints provide real-time monitoring of:\n29| - Database connectivity (MongoDB)\n30| - API endpoints availability\n31| - Data integrity checks\n32| - Service version information\n33| \n34| ### Core Endpoints\n35| \n36| | Endpoint | Method | Description |\n37| |----------|--------|-------------|\n38| | `/api/` | GET | API welcome message |\n39| | `/api/countries` | GET | List all 54 ZLECAf member countries |\n40| | `/api/country-profile/{country_code}` | GET | Get detailed country economic profile |\n41| | `/api/calculate-tariff` | POST | Calculate tariffs between countries |\n42| | `/api/rules-of-origin/{hs_code}` | GET | Get rules of origin for HS code |\n43| | `/api/statistics` | GET | Get comprehensive ZLECAf statistics |\n44| \n45| ### Trade Data Endpoints (NEW)\n46| \n47| | Endpoint | Method | Description |\n48| |----------|--------|-------------|\n49| | `/api/trade-data/latest` | GET | Get latest trade data using smart source selection |\n50| | `/api/trade-data/compare-sources` | GET | Compare all data sources for freshness |\n51| | `/api/trade-data/comtrade/{reporter}/{partner}` | GET | Get UN COMTRADE bilateral trade data directly |\n52| | `/api/trade-data/wto/{reporter}/{partner}` | GET | Get WTO tariff and trade data directly |\n53| \n54| #### Examples:\n55| \n56| **Get Latest Trade Data with Smart Selection:**\n57| ```bash\n58| GET /api/trade-data/latest?reporter=KEN&partner=GHA&hs_code=080300\n59| ```\n60| \n61| **Compare Data Sources:**\n62| ```bash\n63| GET /api/trade-data/compare-sources?countries=KEN&countries=GHA&countries=TZA\n64| ```\n65| \n66| **Direct COMTRADE Access:**\n67| ```bash\n68| GET /api/trade-data/comtrade/KEN/GHA?period=2025&hs_code=080300\n69| ```\n70| \n71| **Direct WTO Access:**\n72| ```bash\n73| GET /api/trade-data/wto/KEN/GHA?product_code=080300\n74| ```\n75| \n76| ## 🏥 Health Monitoring\n77| \n78| ### Health Check Response\n79| \n80| **Endpoint**: `GET /api/health`\n81| \n82| ```json\n83| {\n84|   \"status\": \"healthy\",\n85|   \"service\": \"ZLECAf API\",\n86|   \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n87|   \"timestamp\": \"2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z\"\n88| }\n89| ```\n90| \n91| ### Detailed Status Response\n92| \n93| **Endpoint**: `GET /api/health/status`\n94| \n95| ```json\n96| {\n97|   \"status\": \"healthy\",\n98|   \"timestamp\": \"2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z\",\n99|   \"service\": \"ZLECAf API\",\n100|   \"version\": \"2.0.0\",\n101|   \"checks\": {\n102|     \"database\": {\n103|       \"status\": \"healthy\",\n104|       \"message\": \"MongoDB connection active\"\n105|     },\n106|     \"api_endpoints\": {\n107|       \"status\": \"healthy\",\n108|       \"available_endpoints\": [...]\n109|     },\n110|     \"data\": {\n111|       \"status\": \"healthy\",\n112|       \"countries_count\": 54,\n113|       \"rules_of_origin_sectors\": 97\n114|     }\n115|   }\n116| }\n117| ```\n118| \n119| ## 🔧 Technology Stack\n120| \n121| - **Backend**: FastAPI (Python)\n122| - **Frontend**: React with Shadcn/UI components\n123| - **Database**: MongoDB\n124| - **External APIs**: World Bank API, OEC API, UN COMTRADE API, WTO API\n125| \n126| ## 📦 Data Sources\n127| \n128| - **World Bank** - World Development Indicators (updated daily)\n129| - **UN COMTRADE** - Most recent bilateral trade data (up to 500 calls/day free tier)\n130| - **WTO Data Portal** - Tariff and trade policy data (free access)\n131| - **OEC** - Atlas of Economic Complexity (Observatory of Economic Complexity)\n132| - **UNCTAD** - Tariff data\n133| - **AfDB** - African Economic Outlook\n134| - **IMF** - Regional Economic Outlook\n135| \n136| ### Smart Data Source Selection\n137| \n138| The API automatically selects the best data source based on:\n139| 1. **Data freshness** - Most recent data available\n140| 2. **API availability** - Rate limits and accessibility\n141| 3. **Data coverage** - Specific query requirements\n142| \n143| Priority order: **UN COMTRADE** → **OEC** → **World Bank** → **WTO**\n144| \n145| Data is automatically updated daily at 2:00 AM UTC via GitHub Actions. See [docs/AUTO_UPDATE_DATA.md](docs/AUTO_UPDATE_DATA.md) for details.\n146| \n147| ## 🌍 Coverage\n148| \n149| - **54 African Countries**\n150| - **97 HS2 Sectors** with rules of origin\n151| - **Real-time Trade Data**\n152| - **Economic Projections** through 2030\n153| \n154| ## 🚦 Status Badges Explained\n155| \n156| - ![API Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/API-Online-success) - API is operational\n157| - ![MongoDB](https://img.shields.io/badge/MongoDB-Connected-green) -       "content": "# 🎯 **STRATÉGIE DE COLLECTE DES DONNÉES FISCALES DEPUIS LES DOUANES LOCALES AFRICAINES**\n\nVous avez **100% raison** ! Les données fiscales réelles (DD, TVA, taxes) viennent des **autorités douanières nationales** de chaque pays. C'est beaucoup plus complexe qu'une API centralisée, mais c'est la seule source fiable.\n\n---\n\n## 📋 **INVENTAIRE DES DOUANES DES 54 PAYS AFRICAINS**\n\nVoici un mapping des administrations douanières par pays (sources principales):\n\n```python\n# backend/data_sources/african_customs_registry.py\n\"\"\"\nRegistre complet des administrations douanières des 54 pays africains\n\"\"\"\n\nAFRICAN_CUSTOMS_AUTHORITIES = {\n    # ============ AFRIQUE DU NORD ============\n    \"DZ\": {  # Algérie\n        \"name\": \"Direction Générale des Douanes (DGD)\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.douane.gov.dz\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.douane.gov.dz/tarif-douanier\",\n        \"language\": \"fr\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"html\", \"pdf\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"quarterly\",\n        \"has_api\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.19,\n        \"tva_reduced\": 0.09,\n        \"contact\": \"info@douane.gov.dz\"\n    },\n    \n    \"EG\": {  # Égypte\n        \"name\": \"Egyptian Customs Authority\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.customs.gov.eg\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.customs.gov.eg/CustomsTariff\",\n # Zimbabwe\n        \"name\": \"Zimbabwe Revenue Authority - Customs & Excise\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.zimra.co.zw\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.zimra.co.zw/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=78\",\n        \"language\": \"en\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"pdf\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"annual\",\n        \"has_api\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.15,\n        \"tva_reduced\": None,\n        \"contact\": \"info@zimra.co.zw\"\n    },\n    \n    # ============ AFRIQUE AUSTRALE ============\n    \"BW\": {  # Botswana\n        \"name\": \"Botswana Unified Revenue Service - Customs & Excise\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.burs.org.bw\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.burs.org.bw/index.php/customs-and-excise\",\n        \"language\": \"en\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"pdf\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"annual\",\n        \"has_api\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.12,  # VAT 12%\n        \"tva_reduced\": None,\n        \"regional_bloc\": \"SACU\",\n        \"regional_tariff\": \"SACU Common Tariff\",\n        \"contact\": \"info@burs.org.bw\"\n    },\n    \n    \"SZ\": {  # Eswatini\n        \"name\": \"Eswatini Revenue Authority - Customs & Excise\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.sra.org.sz\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.sra.org.sz/customs\",\n        \"language\": \"en\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"pdf\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"annual\",\n        \"has_api\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.15,\n        \"tva_reduced\": None,\n        \"regional_bloc\": \"SACU\",\n        \"contact\": \"info@sra.org.sz\"\n    },\n    \n    \"LS\": {  # Lesotho\n        \"name\": \"Lesotho Revenue Authority - Customs\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.lra.org.ls\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.lra.org.ls/customs\",\n        \"language\": \"en\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"pdf\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"annual\",\n        \"has_api\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.15,\n        \"tva_reduced\": None,\n        \"regional_bloc\": \"SACU\",\n        \"contact\": \"info@lra.org.ls\"\n    },\n    \n    \"NA\": {  # Namibie\n        \"name\": \"Namibia Revenue Agency - Customs\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.namra.org.na\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.namra.org.na/customs-excise\",\n        \"language\": \"en\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"pdf\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"annual\",\n        \"has_api\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.15,\n        \"tva_reduced\": None,\n        \"regional_bloc\": \"SACU\",\n        \"contact\": \"info@namra.org.na\"\n    },\n    \n    \"ZA\": {  # Afrique du Sud\n        \"name\": \"South African Revenue Service (SARS) - Customs\",\n        \"website\": \"https://www.sars.gov.za\",\n        \"tariff_url\": \"https://www.sars.gov.za/customs-and-excise/trade-statistics/\",\n        \"language\": \"en\",\n        \"data_format\": [\"pdf\", \"excel\", \"api\"],\n        \"update_frequency\": \"monthly\",\n        \"has_api\": True,\n        \"api_endpoint\": \"https://tools.sars.gov.za/tariffsearch/\",\n        \"api_key_required\": False,\n        \"tva_standard\": 0.15,\n        \"tva_reduced\": 0.00,\n        \"regional_bloc\": \"SACU\",\n        \"regional_tariff\": \"SACU Common Tariff\",\n        \"contact\": \"csars@sars.gov.za\"\n    }\n}\n\n# Statistiques du registre\ndef get_registry_stats():\n    \"\"\"Statistiques sur la disponibilité des données\"\"\"\n    total = len(AFRICAN_CUSTOMS_AUTHORITIES)\n    with_website = sum(1 for v in AFRICAN_CUSTOMS_AUTHORITIES.values() if v[\"website\"])\n    with_tariff_url = sum(1 for v in AFRICAN_CUSTOMS_AUTHORITIES.values() if v[\"tariff_url\"])\n    with_api = sum(1 for v in AFRICAN_CUSTOMS_AUTHORITIES.values() if v.get(\"has_api\"))\n    \n    return {\n        \"total_countries\": total,\n        \"with_website\": with_website,\n        \"with_tariff_url\": with_tariff_url,\n        \"with_api\": with_api,\n        \"online_availability_rate\": (with_tariff_url / total) * 100,\n        \"api_availability_rate\": (with_api / total) * 100\n    }\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🏗️ **ARCHITECTURE DE CRAWLING POUR SOURCES HÉTÉROGÈNES**\n\nVu la diversité des sources, voici l'architecture recommandée:\n\n```python\n# backend/crawlers/custom\n\ns_scrapers.py\n\"\"\"\nFactory pattern pour gérer les différents types de scrapers\n\"\"\"\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\nfrom typing import Dict, Any, List\nimport aiohttp\nfrom bs4 import BeautifulSoup\nimport PyPDF2\nimport pandas as pd\nimport re\n\nclass BaseCustomsScraper(ABC):\n    \"\"\"Base pour tous les scrapers douaniers\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, country_code: str, config: Dict[str, Any]):\n        self.country_code = country_code\n        self.config = config\n        self.session = None\n    \n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        self.session = aiohttp.ClientSession(\n            timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=60),\n            headers={\"User-Agent\": \"AfCFTA-DataCrawler/1.0\"}\n        )\n        return self\n    \n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):\n        if self.session:\n            await self.session.close()\n    \n    @abstractmethod\n    async def scrape_tariffs(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Scrape les tarifs douaniers\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    @abstractmethod\n    async def scrape_vat_rates(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Scrape les taux de TVA\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    async def scrape_all(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Scrape toutes les données fiscales\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"country_code\": self.country_code,\n            \"tariffs\": await self.scrape_tariffs(),\n            \"vat\": await self.scrape_vat_rates(),\n            \"scraped_at\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()\n        }\n\n\nclass HTMLTableScraper(BaseCustomsScraper):\n    \"\"\"Pour les sites avec tableaux HTML structurés\"\"\"\n    \n    async def scrape_tariffs(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        url = self.config[\"tariff_url\"]\n        async with self.session.get(url) as response:\n            html = await response.text()\n            soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')\n            \n            tariffs = []\n            # Chercher les tableaux de tarifs\n            tables = soup.find_all('table')\n            \n            for table in tables:\n                rows = table.find_all('tr')\n                for row in rows[1:]:  # Skip header\n                    cells = row.find_all('td')\n                    if len(cells) >= 3:\n                        tariffs.append({\n                            \"hs_code\": cells[0].text.strip(),\n                            \"description\": cells[1].text.strip(),\n                            \"rate\": self._parse_rate(cells[2].text),\n                            \"unit\": cells[3].text.strip() if len(cells) > 3 else None\n                        })\n            \n            return {\"tariff_lines\": tariffs}\n    \n    def _parse_rate(self, rate_str: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Parse un taux (ex: \"15%\", \"15\", \"exempted\")\"\"\"\n        rate_str = rate_str.strip().lower()\n        if \"exempt\" in rate_str or \"free\" in rate_str:\n            return 0.0\n        # Extraire le nombre\n        match = re.search(r'(\\d+\\.?\\d*)', rate_str)\n        if match:\n            return float(match.group(1)) / 100.0\n        return 0.0\n\n\nclass PDFScraper(BaseCustomsScraper):\n    \"\"\"Pour les pays qui publient des PDFs\"\"\"\n    \n    async def scrape_tariffs(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        url = self.config[\"tariff_url\"]\n        \n        # Télécharger le PDF\n        async with self.session.get(url) as response:\n            pdf_content = await response.read()\n        \n        # Parser le PDF (complexe - varie selon structure)\n        # Ici un exemple simplifié\n        import io\n        pdf_reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_content))\n        \n        tariffs = []\n        for page in pdf_reader.pages:\n            text = page.extract_text()\n            # Parser le texte (logique spécifique)\n            lines = text.split('\\n')\n            for line in lines:\n                # Recherche pattern HS code + taux\n                match = re.match(r'(\\d{4,8})\\s+(.+?)\\s+(\\d+\\.?\\d*)%?', line)\n                if match:\n                    tariffs.append({\n                        \"hs_code\": match.group(1),\n                        \"description\": match.group(2).strip(),\n                        \"rate\": float(match.group(3)) / 100.0\n                    })\n        \n        return {\"tariff_lines\": tariffs}\n\n\nclass ExcelScraper(BaseCustomsScraper):\n    \"\"\"Pour les fichiers Excel téléchargeables\"\"\"\n    \n    async def scrape_tariffs(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        url = self.config[\"tariff_url\"]\n        \n        async with self.session.get(url) as response:\n            excel_content = await response.read()\n        \n        # Lire avec pandas\n        import io\n        df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(excel_content))\n        \n        tariffs = []\n        for _, row in df.iterrows():\n            tariffs.append({\n                \"hs_code\": str(row.get(\"HS Code\", row.get(\"Code SH\", \"\"))),\n                \"description\": str(row.get(\"Description\", \"\")),\n                \"rate\": float(row.get(\"Duty Rate\", row.get(\"Taux\", 0))) / 100.0\n            })\n        \n        return {\"tariff_lines\": tariffs}\n\n\nclass APIScraper(BaseCustomsScraper):\n    \"\"\"Pour les rares pays avec API\"\"\"\n    \n    async def scrape_tariffs(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        api_url = self.config[\"api_endpoint\"]\n        headers = {}\n        \n        if self.config.get(\"api_key_required\"):\n            api_key = os.getenv(f\"{self.country_code}_API_KEY\")\n            headers[\"Authorization\"] = f\"Bearer {api_key}\"\n        \n        async with self.session.get(api_url, headers=headers) as response:\n            data = await response.json()\n            return {\"tariff_lines\": data.get(\"tariffs\", [])}\n\n\nclass CustomsScraperFactory:\n    \"\"\"Factory pour créer le bon scraper selon le pays\"\"\"\n    \n    @staticmethod\n    def create_scraper(country_code: str) -> BaseCustomsScraper:\n        config = AFRICAN_CUSTOMS_AUTHORITIES.get(country_code)\n        \n        if not config:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Unknown country code: {country_code}\")\n        \n        # Déterminer le type de scraper nécessaire\n        if config.get(\"has_api\"):\n            return APIScraper(country_code, config)\n        elif \"excel\" in config.get(\"data_format\", []):\n            return ExcelScraper(country_code, config)\n        elif \"pdf\" in config.get(\"data_format\", []):\n            return PDFScraper(country_code, config)\n        elif \"html\" in config.get(\"data_format\", []):\n            return HTMLTableScraper(country_code, config)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f\"No scraper available for {country_code}\")\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 **EXEMPLE D'UTILISATION**\n\n```python\n# backend/jobs/tasks.py\nfrom backend.crawlers.customs_scrapers import CustomsScraperFactory\nfrom backend.db.mongo import db\n\nasync def crawl_country_fiscal_data(job_id: str, country_code: str):\n    \"\"\"Crawl les données fiscales d'un pays\"\"\"\n    \n    try:\n        # Créer le scraper approprié\n        async with CustomsScraperFactory.create_scraper(country_code) as scraper:\n            data = await scraper.scrape_all()\n        \n        # Sauvegarder dans MongoDB\n        db[\"fiscal_data\"].insert_one({\n            **data,\n            \"job_id\": job_id,\n            \"imported_at\": datetime.utcnow()\n        })\n        \n        return {\"success\": True, \"records\": len(data.get(\"tariffs\", {}).get(\"tariff_lines\", []))}\n        \n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to crawl {country_code}: {str(e)}\")\n        return {\"success\": False, \"error\": str(e)}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 📊 **NEXT STEPS**\n\nVoulez-vous que je développe:\n\n1. **Les scrapers spécifiques pour 5 pays pilotes** (ex: Maroc, Kenya, **L'orchestration des crawls** (planification, retry logic, monitoring) ?\n\nDites-moi ce qui est prioritaire pour vous ! 🚀",
